Competitor or Source Name: PADI Open Water Diver
Wording/Vocabulary Notes: What You Learn The PADI Open Water Diver course consists of three main parts: Knowledge Development - online learning to understand basics of dive theory Confined Water Dives - master the required scuba skills for open water Open Water Certification Dives - bring your newly mastered skills to a real diving environment while exploring! Part 1: Knowledge Development Through PADI's Open Water Diver eLearning, you'll learn the facts, principles and concepts needed for diving. You can complete the “classroom” work of the Open Water Diver course at your own pace — anytime, anywhere. The eLearning includes several modules with reading and short videos, followed by quizzes and the final exam. Have a question? Email or call us or set up a time to meet with an instructor. Part 2: Confined Water Dives In a pool or a confined water area, you will learn and master the different dive skills and techniques needed for certification. The number and length of classes can vary based on your schedule and comfort level in the water. You can choose from one of the options below: JOIN OUR SCHEDULED DIVES AT LOCAL POOLS! Join other open water students at the MSSD Pool on Gallaudet University's campus to complete your confined water dives. HAVE ACCESS TO A POOL? We can come to you! Learn in a private setting at the times that work best for you. Have a family member or friend interested in joining? Even better! Contact us for more details. Part 3: Open Water Certification Dives This final portion of the certification includes a minimum four dives in an open water setting (such as a scuba park or ocean) scheduled over at least two days. During these four dives, you will demonstrate mastery of the skills learned in the pool in an open water setting, be involved in dive planning, practice equalizing your ears and practice different methods of safe entries and exits. You can choose from one of the three options below: LOCAL DIVING WEEKEND: Join us for a weekend of diving at a local scuba park. We run these certification weekends at least once each month year-round. Stay close to home while seeing the local underwater sites and meeting some new dive buddies. If this weekend does not work with your schedule, let us know and we'll create a new schedule for you. BLUE PLANET ADVENTURE: You can complete your certification dives during one of our trips to amazing destinations both near and far. Check out our Group Travel page for more details. REFERRAL: If you already have a trip planned, you can complete your open water dives with another PADI shop at your destination. We can provide all of your referral paperwork so you will be ready to complete your open water certification. Be sure to email your request once you have completed the first two parts of the class so we know to send you the paperwork! Prerequisites To enroll in the PADI Open Diver course or Junior Open Water Diver course, you must be: 10 years or older (ages 10-14 can earn the Junior Open Water Diver certification) Complete the required paperwork The Fun Part The fun part about this course is...well, just about all of it because learning to dive is incredible. You breathe underwater for the first time (something you'll never forget) and learn what you need to know to become a certified diver. During the course, you'll make at least five pool dives and four open water dives under the supervision of your Blue Planet Instructor. The Scuba Gear You Use In the PADI Open Water Diver course, you learn to use basic scuba gear including a dive computer and standard accessories. You will need to bring your own swimsuit, towel, mask, fins, snorkel and dive booties. We'll provide everything else. Required Paperwork We'll be sending new students a Smartwaiver to let you fill out all the required paperwork online. This will include a Medical Questionnaire which may require you to get a physician's approval prior to diving. Get Started with the New Diver Orientation! Every student will need to attend a New Diver Orientation sometime before your class meets. These are every Saturday from 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM, but can be set up for other times during busniess hours too. Click here to see our calendar and sign for one today to get started! Optional before you start: Introduction to Open Water Looking for an overview of what the open water training process is really like? Click here to register for the FREE Introduction to Open Water course! This course will teach you everything you need to know before you dive in to the rest your training. PADI Club! All our Open Water Diver Students will be automatically enrolled in PADI Club!  PADI Club Benefits: Annual subscription to Scuba Diving magazine, your ultimate resource for travel inspiration, expert scuba gear reviews, dive training tips, stunning photography and inspiring stories. 20% Off most PADI eLearning - use this to save on Specialty Classes! Access to PADI Club Speaker Series. Free eLearning for PADI ReActivate. And more!

Competitor or Source Name: Learn to Dive - Open Water Diver Course
Wording/Vocabulary Notes: Learn to Dive With SDI’S Open Water Scuba Diver Course Who this course is for: Persons desiring to obtain a Scuba Diving certification Persons interested in underwater exploration Course prerequisites Minimum age 18, 15 with parental consent – Junior Scuba Divers can start at the age of 12, restrictions apply. Must be able to swim and be comfortable in the water. What you can expect to learn: The SDI Open Water Scuba Diver Course will cover all of the necessary skills and knowledge for open water scuba diving, including: The aquatic environment: vision, light, sound, heat loss, tides, currents, waves, surge, etc. Physics and physiology: buoyancy, pressure, air spaces, breathing compressed air, mixed gases, etc. Dive equipment selection, assembly, use and maintenance Dive planning Basic scuba skills including mask and regulator skills, swimming underwater, buoyancy control, basic rescue techniques, and emergency procedures And much more! What’s in it for you: Upon successful completion of the SDI Open Water Scuba Diver course, graduates may: Conduct open water dives in conditions similar to their training Continue their dive education by enrolling in SDI Advanced Diver and Specialty courses SDI Open Water Scuba Diver minimum requirements: Demonstrate mature and sound judgment concerning dive planning and execution Satisfactorily complete the SDI Open Water Scuba Diver knowledge quests or equivalent online learning course Complete all open water requirements safely and efficiently Students must bring their own Mask, Fins and Snorkel to classes. We have a full selection of gear in store, reasonably priced and excellent quality!   Scuba North reserves the right to modify or cancel courses based on weather conditions, staffing, and course enrollment. Each course requires a minimum of 4 students to run as scheduled. In the event of a course cancellation, Scuba North will provide a notice of at least one week, and will either reschedule the student(s) OR provide a refund or store credit.

Competitor or Source Name: Open Water Diver
Wording/Vocabulary Notes: Who should take this course? If you’ve always wanted to take scuba diving lessons, experience unparalleled adventure, and see the world beneath the waves, this is where it starts. Get your scuba diving certification with the SSI Open Water Diver course – This globally-recognized certification program is the best way to begin your life-long adventure as a certified scuba diver. Personalized training is combined with in-water practice sessions to ensure you have the skills and experience required to become truly comfortable underwater. To enroll in a SSI Open Water Diver course or Junior Open Water Diver course, you must be 10 years old or older. You need adequate swimming skills and need to be in good physical health. No prior experience with scuba diving is required. How to get started... Step 1. Create your free diver profile with SSI. Click on the SSI Online Registration link located here. Step 2. Purchase your Open Water Diver Class. Click on the SEE SCHEDULE button located above to select the class that best fits your schedule. Step 3. Download the SSI digital application for your smart device. Android Download or IOS Download Do I need a physician’s approval to dive? You may need a physician’s approval to dive.   To determine if this is needed, complete the Diver Medical Questionnaire.  You can complete this in the App by going to “More”, “Records”, and completing the “Medical”  Statement online.  Or you can download the Diver Medical Participant Questionnaire from our website.  If you answer “Yes” one of the initial 10 questions or “Yes” to one of the follow up questions, you are required to have your physician complete the Physician Evaluation Form prior to any in-water activity.  Please note if you take prescription medications or have had surgery in the last 12 months, you are required to have a physician’s approval.   Please complete the Diver Medical Participant Questionnaire and obtain your Physician's approval.  The third page of the form must be signed by a Physician (MD or DO) and NOT a Nurse, Nurse Practitioner, or a Physician’s Assistant.  You can upload the signed document in the Documents section of your Personal Data or email to support@diverssupplyindy.com.       What Next??? Divers' Supply Indy is notified that you have completed your SSI Online Registration and purchased your Scuba Diving Course. We'll then activate the course, making it available to proceed with the academic portion of your training. Your class will meet according to the schedule provided but you will need to read the digital material and pass the online review questions at the end of each section. Each student is required to provide their own mask, snorkel, and fins for class. We invite you to visit one of our two store locations to be properly fitted by our professional staff for your personal gear. This will ensure the highest degree of comfort while ensuring we don’t exceed your budget or make you late for class. Divers' Supply Indy will provide all the basic scuba gear you’ll use during Phase I, which includes: Regulators, Buoyancy Control Device, Dive Gauges. Weights, and Scuba Tank. Most students prefer to buy their own gear before Phase II, however we do offer a student rental package for everyone completing their Open Water Certifications with Divers’ Supply Indy. Confined Water Training This is where your instructor will put into practice the techniques you learned in the academics program. SSI’s training philosophy is “Comfort through repetition” so you’ll be practicing and perfecting your in-water skills under the supervision and guidance of our instructors and their professional assistants. Each pool session will consist of reviewing any prior skills as well as the introduction of a few new skills, which build upon previous skills. Once you and your instructor feel confident in your abilities to demonstrate the necessary skills for scuba diving in the pool, you’re ready to move on to Phase II – Open Water Certification Dives! The Open Water course is broken into two phases. Phase I · $400.00 Knowledge Development: theory work to help you understand the basic principles of scuba diving. This is reviewed in a final test in which you must score 80% or higher. Test can be retaken if needed. · Confined Water Dives: this is usually done in a pool or shallow water in the sea with the aim of teaching you basic scuba skills. Phase II · $200 additional Open Water Dives: You will complete six open water dives to earn your SSI Open Water Diver certification. These dives will be conducted in a natural environment where you’ll demonstrate your mastery of the skills you learned in the class and perfected in the pool. Only three certification dives can be completed in one day We schedule several opportunities to complete your open water checkout dives year-round. Ask us when and where we’re going to go next! More information Some people prefer to complete their Open Water Checkout Dives during their vacation or in warmer waters. Divers’ Supply Indy can assist you with travel arrangements, finding a referral dive shop, and provide you with all the necessary paperwork to finish your certification in another part of the world. While Referral Divers will pay more to complete their certification in places like Florida, Hawaii, or the Caribbean; we offer a two Phase certification program to give you the most custom and individualized experience available. We love the chance to talk with people about diving. If you have a moment, come to our location and ask us for an orientation to diving with us. We will sit down and review where you want to dive, what you want to accomplish, and create a personalized training program and equipment list that’s capable of being completed in small bites or all at once. We look forward to working with you soon!

Wording/Vocabulary Notes: PADI ReActivate™ Scuba Refresher Program Get Back in the Water with Confidence – Signature Scuba, Rancho Cucamonga Haven’t Been Diving in a While? Let’s Get You ReActivated. Whether it’s been 6 months or 6 years since your last dive, the PADI ReActivate™ Program at Signature Scuba is your personalized refresher to rebuild your skills, boost your confidence, and safely return to the underwater world. Perfect for certified divers who need a quick review before an upcoming trip or just want to feel comfortable back in the gear again. ReActivate your PADI certification and get a new eCard that shows dive operators you’re ready to explore again—refreshed and re-qualified. What’s Included in PADI ReActivate™: PADI eLearning review of key concepts, safety protocols, and dive planning Confined water session with a pro to review gear setup, buoyancy, emergency skills, and more Optional open water dive (based on your travel or training goals) Updated PADI certification eCard showing your ReActivated status One-on-one or small group instruction at your pace Ideal For: Certified divers who’ve been out of the water for a year or more Travelers preparing for a dive vacation Local divers returning after time off Divers wanting to rebuild comfort with equipment and skills Anyone who’s unsure if they’re “dive ready” Why ReActivate with Signature Scuba? At Signature Scuba in Rancho Cucamonga, we specialize in supportive, skills-focused refresher training that’s tailored to your unique experience level. We make it easy to get back in the water—whether you're returning after a break, preparing for a big dive trip, or just want a local tune-up before heading to Catalina, Laguna, or La Jolla. Our dive pros work with students from Riverside County, Pasadena, Orange County, and the wider Inland Empire, providing top-notch instruction in a safe, welcoming environment. Your Dive Journey Isn’t Over—It’s Just ReActivating. Scuba diving is a lifelong skill—but even the best divers need a tune-up. Let’s get you back underwater with the skills, knowledge, and confidence to enjoy every dive. Call or visit Signature Scuba today to schedule your PADI ReActivate™ session!

Competitor or Source Name: PADI ReActivate
Wording/Vocabulary Notes: Update Your Date – PADI ReActivate Turn your "old" certification into a current one. If you are a PADI diver who hasn't been in the water for a while, you might be worried that your skills—and your certification card—look a little out of date. The PADI ReActivate program is the most comprehensive way to refresh your knowledge and skills. Unlike a standard pool check, this program includes PADI E-Learning to review dive theory on your tablet or phone, followed by an in-water session. Best of all? Upon completion, you receive a new certification card with a "ReActivated" date on it, proving to dive operators worldwide that you are current and ready to dive. Why Choose PADI ReActivate? Get a Fresh Card: This is the only refresher that issues a new PADI certification card with a current date, eliminating questions at dive shops about when you last dived. Review Theory Online: Brush up on physics, physiology, and dive planning at your own pace with PADI’s interactive mobile app before you even arrive at the shop. Customize Your Skills: In the water, you will demonstrate the basics, but you can also ask your instructor to focus on specific areas where you feel rusty (like deploying an SMB or hovering). Digital Convenience: Access your refreshed certification instantly through the PADI App. The ReActivate Process Digital Learning: Complete the PADI ReActivate E-Learning at home. It uses realistic scenarios to verify your understanding of dive safety concepts. Pool Session: Join a Space Coast Dive Center professional in the pool to practice gear assembly, mask clearing, buoyancy control, and emergency procedures. Certification: We process your completion, and PADI issues your updated digital card. Who Is This For? PADI Divers: Specifically designed for divers who hold a PADI Open Water (or higher) certification. Travelers: Perfect if you are planning a trip to a destination that strictly enforces "currency" requirements. Safety-Conscious Divers: If you want a structured, thorough review of both the mental and physical aspects of diving. Expand Your Range Want the real salt water experience? While ReActivate covers the pool work, nothing beats the ocean. You can upgrade your experience by adding our 2-Tank Guided Boat Dive package to lock in your skills with an instructor by your side on the reef.

Teaching staff will be at the store 30 minutes prior to and after class. Camp fee: $695 (includes: Book kit, rental gear, pool and park transportation and fees) Registration for the course is required before the SCUBA camp start date. Parents must be available for approximately 15 minutes on Monday prior to the start of class. Parents will meet the instructor(s), paperwork, class fees, and medicals (if necessary) will be collected at this time. A medical questionnaire must be filled out for each student and, if conditions warrant, a physician’s approval will be required. Each student will need to supply his/her personal gear, which includes: Mask Snorkel Fins & booties (approximate cost $200) Students will also need to bring a towel, bathing suit, and a desire to learn in a fun and exciting atmosphere. Each student may bring a sack lunch and drink or have the option of walking to Subway sandwich shop with the camp staff to purchase a lunch (please bring sufficient funds). Schedule: Class and pool sessions M-W Open water dives: Th & F The pool sessions are conducted at William H. Williams memorial pool located in Webster. Open water dives are conducted at a local lake within about a 30-minute drive from the Clear Lake area. Transportation to the pool and open water dives will be provided. Parents, you are welcome to attend with your child.

Service/Page Category: DAN DFA Pro / CPR / First Aid / AED / Emergency Oxygen training
Competitor or Source Name: DAN: Diving First Aid (DFA)
Wording/Vocabulary Notes: A dive emergency is rarely a single event. More often than not, separate small problems compound to create a larger emergency. The Fun Part The fun part about this training is learning scuba specific emergeny skills that allow you to become a more confident and compentent diver both above and below the surface. What You Learn The course includes the knowledge and skill development from each of the following DAN courses: Basic Life Support: CPR and First Aid Emergency Oxygen for Scuba Diving Injuries Neurological Assessment First Aid for Hazardous Marine Life Injuries In total, this accounts for knowledge and skill development of the following: CPR & use of AEDs Neurological assessments Decompression illness and stroke Atmospheric gases Respiration and circulation Oxygen safety Oxygen first aid for scuba diving injuries Envenomations from marine life Traumatic injuries Life-threatening complications Seafood poisonings Avoidance of marine life injuries Prerequisites To enroll in the course you must: Care about other people and want to be able to act in a medical emergency The Scuba Gear You Use Scuba gear is not necessary to take this course. The Learning Materials You Need Once you are confirmed for the course, your instructor will email you access to DAN's comprehensive online training platform. 100% of the online class needs to be completed before the in-person training can begin, so be sure to start it right away!   Your Next Adventure After completing the DFA course, take the PADI Rescue Diver course. Together, these courses round out your ability to handle scuba diver emergencies. For more information about this or other DAN or PADI courses have a chat with one of the Blue Planet Team members.
